source_notes.title: snyk/agent-scan: Security scanner for AI agents, MCP servers and agent skills
source_notes.excerpt: Discover and scan agent components on your machine for prompt injections and vulnerabilities (including agents, MCP servers, skills). Detects Prompt Injection, Malware Payloads, Untrusted Content, Credential Handling, Hardcoded Secrets in skills.

source_notes.title: Securing the Agent Skill Ecosystem: How Snyk and Vercel Are Locking Down the New Software Supply Chain
source_notes.excerpt: When a new agent skill is installed using the npx skills installer, Vercel's infrastructure triggers a call to Snyk's high-throughput scanning API... possible to deploy this as an automated gate at submission time — you can block truly malicious skills. Language understanding to catch prompt injection and natural language malware that hides in the SKILL.md instructions.

source_notes.title: Introducing Agent Scan - Skill Inspector: Detect Malicious Skills Instantly
source_notes.excerpt: Agent Scan analyzes AI skills as a combination of Skill code and Natural language instructions to identify security risks before installation: prompt injection, malicious code, suspicious downloads, hardcoded secrets, improper credential handling.

source_notes.title: ToxicSkills: Snyk Finds Prompt Injection in 36% of Agent Skills
source_notes.excerpt: Scanned 3,984 agent skills from ClawHub using the mcp-scan engine... 36.82% (1,467 skills) have at least one security flaw... 76 malicious payloads designed for credential theft and data exfiltration... hidden instructions, obfuscated commands, and credential exposure within SKILL.md documentation.

source_notes.title: Introducing MCP-Scan: Protecting MCP with Invariant
source_notes.excerpt: MCP-Scan checks installed MCP servers for prompt injections, tool poisoning, and cross-origin escalations. Built-in Tool Pinning detects MCP Rug Pull attacks, verifying integrity of installed tools by tracking changes via tool hashing.

source_notes.title: Hidden Unicode Instruction Injection in AI Agent Skills
source_notes.excerpt: CI/CD pipelines should incorporate Unicode linting that rejects any skill file, MCP server manifest, or AI configuration file containing Tags-block codepoints or anomalous zero-width character densities. Recommends NFKC normalization as a preprocessing defense before tokenization.

source_notes.title: Scary Agent Skills: Hidden Unicode Instructions in Skills ...And How To Catch Them
source_notes.excerpt: Invisible Unicode Tag codepoints embedded in skill files are interpreted as instructions by Gemini, Claude, Grok... survive human review. AID Scanner (wunderwuzzi23/aid) scans files and flags suspicious Unicode sequences. I have seen Skill scanners lacking this capability.

source_notes.title: Introducing the Agent Governance Toolkit: Open-source runtime security for AI agents
source_notes.excerpt: Brings runtime security governance to autonomous AI agents... a stateless policy engine that intercepts every agent's action before execution at sub-millisecond latency (<0.1ms p99). Governs autonomous decision-making as agents execute.

source_notes.title: NeMo Guardrails Configuration & Architecture
source_notes.excerpt: Operates as a proxy layer: Request -> Input Rails -> LLM -> Output Rails -> Response. Input rails handle jailbreak detection, prompt injection filtering, content moderation at runtime.

source_notes.title: Llama Guard: LLM-based Input-Output Safeguard for Human-AI Conversations
source_notes.excerpt: An LLM-based input-output safeguard model... classifies content in both LLM inputs (prompt classification) and in LLM responses (response classification) at inference across 13 hazard categories.

source_notes.title: Lakera Guard: Runtime visibility and protection for AI applications
source_notes.excerpt: A real-time AI application firewall... screens any user or reference contents passed into an LLM and the output response in real time. Detects prompt injection, jailbreaks, data leakage with sub-50ms latency (vendor self-report).

source_notes.title: NVIDIA/garak: the LLM vulnerability scanner
source_notes.excerpt: Probes a deployed LLM endpoint for prompt injection, jailbreaks, hallucination, data leakage via 50+ probe modules and 23 generator backends; evaluates model responses (dynamic/runtime probing of a live model).

source_notes.title: Semgrep Custom Rules & CI deployment
source_notes.excerpt: Custom YAML rules with pattern/severity; native CI plugins can block pull requests and fail builds when findings exceed threshold. Covers 30+ programming languages — code/IaC SAST, not agent-artefact markdown semantics.

source_notes.title: OSV-Scanner usage & CI gating
source_notes.excerpt: Connects a project's dependency list with known vulnerabilities from osv.dev; reusable GitHub workflows fail builds above a severity threshold. Operates on dependency manifests/SBOMs, not agent instruction artefacts.

source_notes.title: Socket: Block zero-day supply chain attacks
source_notes.excerpt: Static analysis flags new install scripts, obfuscated code, privileged API usage in dependencies; Firewall proxy blocks malicious package downloads at install time. Targets package supply chain, not markdown agent artefacts.

source_notes.title: GitHub Artifact Attestations (SLSA build provenance + Sigstore)
source_notes.excerpt: SLSA build provenance is a signed document making claims about how an artifact was built; Sigstore provides keyless signing and Rekor transparency log. Proves provenance/integrity — does not inspect artefact content for injection.

claims.statement: Build/submission-time, fail-closed gating of markdown agent skill files (SKILL.md) for prompt injection and supply-chain threats already exists as a shipped commercial product (Snyk Agent Scan + Vercel/npx skills installer).

evidence.quote: When a new agent skill is installed using the npx skills installer, Vercel's infrastructure triggers a call to Snyk's high-throughput scanning API... possible to deploy this as an automated gate at submission time — you can block truly malicious skills.

Agent Scan analyzes AI skills as a combination of Skill code and Natural language instructions to identify security risks before installation: prompt injection, malicious code, suspicious downloads, hardcoded secrets.

claims.falsifier: If Snyk/Vercel gating were shown to be advisory-only (reports without blocking) and no other product blocked malicious skills pre-deploy, the claim would fail.

claims.statement: The majority of the named guardrail tools (NeMo Guardrails, Llama Guard, Lakera Guard, Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit) operate at runtime/inference on model I/O or agent actions, not on pre-deploy artefacts.

evidence.quote: Operates as a proxy layer: Request -> Input Rails -> LLM -> Output Rails -> Response. Input rails handle jailbreak detection, prompt injection filtering at runtime.
a stateless policy engine that intercepts every agent's action before execution at sub-millisecond latency (<0.1ms p99).
a real-time AI application firewall... screens any user or reference contents passed into an LLM and the output response in real time.

claims.falsifier: If any of these tools documented a primary build-time/static artefact-gating mode, the runtime-centric framing would weaken.

claims.statement: Invariant mcp-scan (the engine inside Snyk Agent Scan) statically scans MCP/agent artefacts for prompt injection and tool poisoning before use and pins tool hashes to detect post-approval tampering (rug pulls).

evidence.quote: MCP-Scan checks installed MCP servers for prompt injections, tool poisoning... Built-in Tool Pinning detects MCP Rug Pull attacks, verifying integrity of installed tools by tracking changes via tool hashing.
Discover and scan agent components on your machine for prompt injections and vulnerabilities (including agents, MCP servers, skills).

claims.falsifier: If tool-pinning/hashing were removed or shown to be runtime-only, the integrity-checking overlap would be weaker.

claims.statement: Authoritative guidance already prescribes build-time CI/CD linting of agent skill files for hidden-Unicode prompt-injection smuggling — i.e., the exact 'hidden-unicode smuggling lint' agent-enterprise claims.

evidence.quote: CI/CD pipelines should incorporate Unicode linting that rejects any skill file, MCP server manifest, or AI configuration file containing Tags-block codepoints or anomalous zero-width character densities.
AID Scanner (wunderwuzzi23/aid) scans files and flags suspicious Unicode sequences... I have seen Skill scanners lacking this capability.

claims.falsifier: If no published standard/tool recommended pre-deploy Unicode linting of skill files, agent-enterprise's lint would be novel.

claims.statement: The agent-skill supply-chain attack surface is empirically real and active (not hypothetical), validating the problem agent-enterprise targets even if the tooling is not novel.

evidence.quote: Scanned 3,984 agent skills from ClawHub... 36.82% (1,467 skills) have at least one security flaw... 76 malicious payloads designed for credential theft and data exfiltration.
production agent platforms... will execute arbitrary shell commands and curl requests embedded as invisible instructions inside agent skill markdown files, surviving any visual code review.

claims.falsifier: If independent re-scans found near-zero malicious skills, the threat's materiality would be in doubt.

claims.statement: The dependency-scanning, SBOM, and provenance/attestation sub-components of agent-enterprise's safety layer are commodity capabilities already provided fail-closed in CI by OSV-Scanner, Socket, Semgrep, and SLSA/Sigstore.

evidence.quote: reusable GitHub workflows... fail builds above a severity threshold.
Static analysis flags new install scripts, obfuscated code, privileged API usage; Firewall proxy blocks malicious package downloads at install time.
SLSA build provenance is a signed document making claims about how an artifact was built; Sigstore provides keyless signing and Rekor transparency log.

claims.falsifier: If these tools could not be wired to fail builds, the dep/provenance overlap would be weaker.

claims.statement: garak and promptfoo are model/app red-team evaluators (they probe a live model and can fail a CI build on regression), not static gates over the agent-artefact source files themselves.

evidence.quote: Probes a deployed LLM endpoint for prompt injection, jailbreaks... evaluates model responses (dynamic probing of a live model).

claims.falsifier: If garak/promptfoo added a static SKILL.md content linter, the distinction would collapse.

claims.statement: agent-enterprise's residual novelty is not any individual primitive (all of which exist) but the unified build-time resolution pipeline that combines artefact injection-linting + trust-labelling/quarantine + checksums + SBOM + dep-scan with a fail-closed gate over multi-platform agent deploy artefacts.

evidence.quote: The article mentions a Security Verified badge as a transparency signal but does not describe checksums, quarantine systems, or granular trust labeling mechanisms beyond this visual indicator.
Built-in Tool Pinning... verifying integrity of installed tools by tracking changes via tool hashing.

claims.falsifier: If a single shipped tool already bundles artefact injection-lint + quarantine + checksum + SBOM + dep-scan as one fail-closed build pipeline for agent artefacts, this residual novelty disappears.

claims.statement: The claim that 'build-time fail-closed validation of agent artefacts against prompt-injection + supply-chain' is a NOVEL defensible niche is overhyped as stated.

evidence.quote: identify security risks before installation: prompt injection, malicious code, suspicious downloads, hardcoded secrets, improper credential handling.
CI/CD pipelines should incorporate Unicode linting that rejects any skill file... containing Tags-block codepoints.

claims.falsifier: Evidence that no shipped tool gates markdown agent artefacts pre-deploy for injection would restore novelty.

claims.statement: The specific structural defence of build-time data/instruction separation via a nonce data-fence in resolved agent artefacts was not found in any surveyed competitor and is a candidate genuine differentiator.

evidence.quote: language understanding to catch prompt injection and natural language malware that hides in the SKILL.md instructions.
recommends Unicode normalization (NFKC form) as a preprocessing defense layer before tokenization.

claims.falsifier: Surveyed tools detect/normalise injection but none documents emitting a nonce-delimited data fence into the resolved artefact; finding one would falsify this.
